# SniffPal — LLM Context File
# This file helps AI assistants understand the codebase quickly.

## What this project is
SniffPal is a browser-based network intelligence tool. It parses packet captures (Wireshark JSON or scapy output) and shows device analysis, security findings, and a health score. 100% client-side — no backend for the web version.

## Two modes
1. Web mode — user uploads a capture file, analysis runs in browser
2. Pi mode — Flask server on Raspberry Pi serves the app + captures automatically

## Key architectural decisions
- Parser runs in a Web Worker (src/core/parser.worker.js) — never blocks UI
- src/core/ = shared logic for all distributions (web, pi, future android)
- src/components/ = React UI, web-only
- pi/ = Flask server, Pi-only
- scapy-capture-tool/ = Python capture script, Pi/Linux

## Detection rules (finding IDs)
- PRIV_MDNS_001 — mDNS name/service broadcast
- PRIV_SSDP_001 — SSDP UPnP discovery
- PRIV_LLMNR_001 — LLMNR hostname broadcast
- PRIV_NBNS_001 — NetBIOS name broadcast
- IOT_TEL_001 — MQTT cleartext (TCP 1883) or CoAP (UDP 5683)
- IOT_UPNP_002 — UPnP IGD port punching (planned)
- IOT_C2_001 — C2 beacon / no-DNS outbound (planned)

## Pi server API
- GET  /api/status          — server status + settings
- GET  /api/digests         — last 48 digests
- GET  /api/digests/latest  — most recent capture
- POST /api/capture/start   — trigger manual capture
- GET  /api/settings        — current settings
- POST /api/settings        — update interval + interface

## Tech constraints (do not change)
- Vite pinned at 5.4.1 — v6/v8 caused breakage
- Tailwind v3 — v4 uses different CSS syntax, breaks build
- No new heavy npm dependencies without checking bundle size first
- scapy requires sudo on Linux for raw sockets

## Current version
v2.1.1 — Pi mode UI, src/core/ restructure, PDF fix, Pi server

## Repo
https://github.com/kannanokannan/sniffpal
